response:
```python
import pytest

from your_module import validate_password  # Replace with the actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    ("password", "expected"),
    [
        # Happy path
        ("Abcdefg1", True),          # exactly 8 chars
        ("StrongPass123", True),     # longer valid password
        ("A1bcdef!", True),          # symbols are allowed
        ("Abc def1", True),          # spaces are allowed

        # Edge cases: length
        ("", False),
        ("A1b", False),
        ("Abcdef1", False),         # 7 chars, otherwise valid
        ("Abcdefg", False),         # 7 chars, missing digit

        # Missing required character classes
        ("abcdefg1", False),        # missing uppercase
        ("ABCDEFG1", False),        # missing lowercase
        ("Abcdefgh", False),        # missing digit
        ("12345678", False),        # missing upper and lower
        ("!!!!!!!!", False),        # missing upper, lower, and digit
    ],
)
def test_validate_password(password, expected):
    assert validate_password(password) is expected


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "invalid_password",
    [
        None,
        12345678,
    ],
)
def test_validate_password_invalid_type_raises_type_error(invalid_password):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        validate_password(invalid_password)
```
